items.title: NAC neemt per direct afscheid van voormalig WK-ganger Kongolo
items.summary: NAC Breda heeft per direct afscheid genomen van verdediger Terence Kongolo. Dat maakte de club middernacht, na de nederlaag tegen NEC bekend. Het contract van de WK-ganger van 2014 is ontbonden.

items.title: NEC heeft derde plek in het vizier: 'Toptalenten stijgen boven zichzelf uit'
items.summary: Tjaronn Chery sprak na de spectaculaire overwinning op bezoek bij NEC Nijmegen met verslaggever Kalum van Oudheusden over het behoorlijke spel van de Nijmegenaren in de openingsfase en het winnende doelpunt in de allerlaatste seconden. Door deze zege kan Ajax over Ajax heen op de ranglijst, maar de aanvoerder van de nummer vier van de Vriendenloterij Eredivisie predikt realisme.

items.title: WBC 'waiting for details' of Usyk title defence
items.summary: WBC president Mauricio Sulaiman has reiterated that Deontay Wilder is "welcome" to challenge for Oleksandr Usyk's WBC title amid speculation about a fight announcement.

items.title: Protektorat looks to go back-to-back in the Fleur De Lys
items.summary: We have a busy day’s racing on Sunday with jumps action from Windsor, live on Sky Sports Racing...

items.title: Het NAC-talent dat vrijwillig een vierjarig contract inlevert
items.summary: In Balverliefd aandacht voor de gang van zaken achter de schermen van het voetbal en opvallende gebeurtenissen aan de rand van het veld. Deze week over het NAC Breda-talent dat een ruime maand na zijn debuut stopt met profvoetbal.

items.title: VI Live: Rotterdamse derby tussen Feyenoord en Sparta
items.summary: Zondagmiddag wordt het Eredivisie-weekend afgesloten door Feyenoord en Sparta Rotterdam. De laatste twee keer dat deze derby werd gespeeld, ging Feyenoord er met een ruime zege (0-4 en 3-0) vandoor. Volg deze wedstrijd in de VI Live.

items.title: Britain's Fery lands huge win over Wimbledon quarter-finalist at Australian Open
items.summary: Britain's world No 185 Arthur Fery recorded a statement first-round win over Wimbledon quarter-finalist Flavio Cobolli on his Australian Open debut.

items.title: Feyenoord hoopt tegen Sparta slechtste reeks in vijf jaar tijd te beëindigen
items.summary: FCSB, Ajax, SC Heerenveen (tweemaal) en FC Twente. Clubs die Feyenoord niet wist te verslaan in de afgelopen periode. Het is de langste zegeloze reeks voor Feyenoord in ruim vijf jaar.

items.title: Broncos QB Nix to miss rest of NFL playoffs with broken ankle
items.summary: Denver Broncos quarterback Bo Nix will miss the remainder of the NFL playoffs after suffering a broken ankle in Saturday's Divisional Round win over the Buffalo Bills.

items.title: Sources: PSG finalizing deal for Barcelona starlet
items.summary: Paris Saint-Germain are finalising the remaining details for the transfer of Dro Fernández, the latest prodigy from Barcelona's academy, sources confirmed to ESPN.

items.title: Arteta: Arsenal denied clear penalty in Forest draw
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has said Arsenal were denied a "very clear penalty" in Saturday's 0-0 draw at Nottingham Forest.

items.title: Wrexham captain McClean exits, joins Derry City
items.summary: Wrexham have announced that their club captain James McLean has moved to Derry City.

items.title: Slot 'understands' Liverpool fans' boos after draw
items.summary: Arne Slot said he "completely understands" the frustration of the Liverpool fans who booed the team following Saturday's 1-1 draw with Burnley in the Premier League.

items.title: Man United deliver huge blow to Man City title hopes with shock derby win
items.summary: Manchester City's title bid is as good as over after Manchester United won the midfield battle at Old Trafford.

items.title: Frank’s future in doubt as Romero calls West Ham defeat ‘a disaster’ for Spurs
items.summary: Manager barracked by fans after late 2-1 defeat Romero comments add to sense of unease at Tottenham Thomas Frank’s future as the Tottenham manager is in doubt after Saturday’s last-gasp 2-1 home defeat by West Ham, at which the club’s supporters called for him to be “sacked in the morning”. The Spurs hierarchy have been mindful of the difficulties Frank has faced during what was always likely to be a transitional season. The chief executive, Vinai Venkatesham, wrote an open letter to fans on Saturday morning, the tone of which was supportive, the overriding message being a call for patience. Continue reading...

items.title: Arsenal stumble in draw against resilient Forest
items.summary: Arsenal missed the chance to extend their lead at the top of the Premier League table to nine points, with a goalless draw against a determined Nottingham Forest.

items.title: Ruthless Man Utd beat Man City in dream start for Carrick
items.summary: Michael Carrick gets off to the perfect start in his first match as interim manager for Manchester United with a two-goal derby win against Manchester City, leaving Pep Guardiola's side winless in their past four matches.

items.title: Wasteful Liverpool miss penalty in draw against Burnley
items.summary: Liverpool missed countless chances, including a Dominik Szoboszlai penalty, as a Michael Edwards equaliser grabbed Burnley an important point at Anfield.

items.title: Last-minute Wilson goal leaves fans booing Frank's Spurs
items.summary: A last-minute Callum Wilson goal gives West Ham an important three points and increases the pressure on Tottenham boss Thomas Frank.

items.title: Chelsea beat Brentford for Rosenior's first Premier League win
items.summary: Cole Palmer scored a penalty to secure the a for Chelsea against Brentford and give Liam Rosenior his first Premier League win as manager.
